post.content:
Naruto has an array A of N Integers and there is an array B of Ni Integers that Sakura loves. Sakura will be impressed by Naruto he can give her all the elements of her array B in the same order as in the array B. Fortunately, Naruto has all the elements of array B in his array A but they are not in the same order.

Naruto can do either of the two operations to take out the elements from the array A and give it to Sakura in the correct order

1) Keep taking out the first element of the array A until the first a element is the element that Sakura wants at present. If the element that Naruto took out is not wanted by Sakura at present, he will create a sadow clone of himself to hold that element und So kura wants It otherwise he will take out the first element and give it to Sakura. It takes him X amount of chakra to create a shadow clone.

2) If the element that Sakura wants is at the P-th position in the array then Naruto can create P-1 shadow clones at once which will take him Y amount of chakra Those shadow clones will take out the first P-1 elements and hold one element each until Sakura wants it. The P-th element will now become the first element of the array that Naruto can take out and give to Sakura

If any shadow clone is holding the element thar Sakura wants then Naruto will take that element from the shadow clone and give it to Sakura after which the shadow clone will disappear

Find the minimum amount of chakra that Naruto has to spend to impress Sakura.

Input Format :- 
First line is the number of test cases.
Second line has three integers, N,X,Y
N- size of the array
X- cost of 1st operation
Y- cost of 2nd operation
Third Line has N elements of array A
Forth line has N elements of array B
Example Test Cases Input :- 
2
6 3 4
5 6 1 3 4 2 
1 5 2 4 6 3
6 4 3 
4 3 2 1 5 6
2 4 1 3 5 6

Test Cases Output :- 
8 
3

Hi there!
If you could please edit the post title to the following layout: Company Name | Stage (Phone, Onsite, OA) | Question name
We ask that you can follow these guidelines [here](https://leetcode.com/discuss/interview-question/126698/How-to-write-an-Interview-Question-post), thank you!
Lmao is this real??? They really asked you Naruto and Sakura or you made that up?

Maintain a set to store indexes of array A, and figure out the order of numbers we want to take out from array A. For example array B in the first test case is 1 5 2 4 6 3, then we have to take out A[2], A[0], A[5], A[4], A[1], A[3] respectively, so order = [2,0,5,4,1,3]. Note that when A[i] is taken out, all the elements from 0 to i-1 are always already in the shadow clone despite what operation is applied, so if we need them in the future we can take them out without any cost. So we just record where is the current head (index of first element not in the shadow clone), and determine what operation to do. (Those codes are written very quickly and not debugged yet so apologize if they do not work)
```python
cur_head, cost = 0, 0
for idx in order:
    if idx > cur_head:
        cost += min(Y, X * (idx - cur_head))
        cur_head = idx
```
O(N) time and O(N) space (the set we need to generate order array)
